headline: France's military spending plans face reality of fragile national finances
description: The government's defense objectives are incompatible with meeting European public spending commitments, France's main audit institution has warned, pointing to 'certainly difficult choices' ahead.

headline: Iranians express bitterness at war: 'The jailer hasn't changed, my friends, and we are all in line for death'
Giorgia Meloni and the defeat of ideology
Ukraine gains upper hand in aerial war against Russia
description: Powered by a new generation of drones, Ukrainian forces carried out a record number of strikes in March. Russian oil and gas infrastructure became a primary target, impacting global energy prices.

headline: Melania Trump blasts 'lies' linking her to Epstein: 'I am not his victim'
description: Jeffrey Epstein died in federal custody in 2019 while awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges involving minors, but the scandal has repeatedly overshadowed Donald Trump's second presidency. 'I was not a participant. Was never on Epstein's plane, and never visited his private island,' the first lady said.

headline: In Budapest, a seven-hour concert aims to hasten Viktor Orban's 'end of the regime'
description: Robert Puzser, an intellectual and outspoken critic of the Fidesz party led by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, is organizing this historic free show on Friday, April 10, to urge citizens to turn out for Sunday's legislative elections.

headline: AI is reshaping translators' work: 'Translation isn't simply converting words from one language to another'
description: New skills are needed, pricing pressure is mounting and 'post-editing' is on the rise. The rise of artificial intelligence is transforming the translation industry and training programs must adapt.

World Press Photo 2026: Abdulmonam Eassa honored for his coverage of the war in Sudan for Le Monde
description: French-Syrian photojournalist Abdulmonam Eassa received the prestigious World Press Photo award on Thursday in the 'Stories' category for his photo report 'War in Sudan, A Nation Trapped,' carried out in October 2024 and December 2025 for Le Monde.

headline: Three writers on how Simone de Beauvoir influenced their relationship with feminism
description: For the 40th anniversary of the philosopher's death, French writers Louise Chennevière, Pauline Delabroy-Allard and Blandine Rinkel share how Beauvoir has impacted them with Le Monde.
byline: Blandine Rinkel ,  Louise Chennevière and  Pauline Delabroy-Allard

headline: Trump's escalatory rhetoric raises questions over his mental health
description: Members of Congress, stunned by the crude and genocidal language used by the president, have begun to discuss the US Constitution's 25th Amendment, which allows for the president to be declared unfit and for executive powers to be transferred to the vice president.
